title.rendered: The Winter Blanket: Darkness on the Edge of Town

content.rendered:
On one level, this cover has no business working as well as it does.

“Darkness on the Edge of Town” is an angry song–anger born out of frustration.

But if we listen to Bruce’s lyrics carefully, we realize that while Bruce sings “Darkness” in a tone of determination and defiance, at its core it’s a song of addiction and defeat.

Midwest band The Winter Blanket gets it. Their cover taps into the isolation and resignation of the point-of-view character and reinvents the song as one of quiet contemplation and acceptance of one’s lot in life.

The result is a delicate, introspective recasting of one of Bruce’s most well-known songs, by a band that clearly understands and honors Bruce’s songwriting.

Take a listen:

© March 20, 2019

excerpt.rendered: The Winter Blanket gives us a quiet, introspective take of “Darkness on the Edge of Town” that conveys the resignation and defeat lurking at the heart of the song.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Darkness on the Edge of Town
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: "Darkness on the Edge of Town" is one of Bruce's most enduring classics. But is it a song about heroic defiance or defeat and addiction?

jetpack-related-posts.title: Introduction
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce Springsteen's fourth studio album becomes the first to receive the complete Roll of the Dice treatment.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Sax Song
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Still missing in action after all these years, "Down by the River" is a straight up rocker featuring the E Street Band in all its glory that would have been right at home on The River.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Something in the Night
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: It started out as a romantic escape song and evolved into a cry of despair. Let's trace the evolution of this Darkness masterpiece.

jetpack-related-posts.title: July 1
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: The Born in the U.S.A. Tour gets underway, and the Reunion Tour comes to a close. Plus: catch guest spots with Brian Wilson and La Bamba, and listen to the E Street Band tinker with "New York City Serenade" during a Darkness soundcheck.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Factory
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: "Factory" ranks among the best of Bruce's early song-writing, with lyrical economy and an instrumental track both spare and ornate--it's a marvel of restraint and emotional bravery.
